issue-1.description: Props interface uses `any[]` instead of the imported `Todo[]` type. The `Todo` type is already imported on line 1 but is unused because the props interface declares `todos: any[]` instead of `todos: Todo[]`. This defeats TypeScript's static analysis, meaning any object shape could be passed to this component without compile-time errors.
issue-1.suggestion: Change `todos: any[]` to `todos: Todo[]` in the TodoListProps interface to use the already-imported `Todo` type.

issue-2.description: useSWR is called with an external URL (`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos`) as the key instead of an internal API route path. The skill's key convention rule states: "Key convention: use the API route path (`/api/products`, `/api/products/${id}`)." While the shared fetcher and global SWRConfig are already properly configured (lib/fetcher.ts and pages/_app.tsx), calling external APIs directly from the client bypasses any backend control over data transformation, error normalization, caching, and rate limiting.
issue-2.suggestion: Create an internal API route at `pages/api/todos/index.ts` that proxies the external API call, then change the SWR key from `'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/todos'` to `'/api/todos'`. This keeps the external dependency behind the backend and follows the project's key convention pattern.
